e20_field_comparator.note: Q(sqrt2) shared with dihedral -> field match cannot by itself pin 2O

reason: the silver hearing group does NOT show 2O structure and CANNOT: its mod-8 shadow is SL(2,Z/8) (order 384, not 48); the only order-48 natural quotient SL(2,Z/4) is not 2O (it has zero order-8 elements and 7 involutions, whereas 2O has 12 order-8 elements and a UNIQUE involution -- the binary-polyhedral signature); and 2O is never SL(2,q) for any prime power q. The shadow mechanism yields a binary polyhedral group only at ODD-PRIME conductors (SL(2,F3)=2T at 3, SL(2,F5)=2I at 5, each with a unique involution); the silver conductor is 8 (even, =2^3), whose shadow SL(2,Z/8) has 15 involutions and is not binary polyhedral at all. So the metallic ladder does NOT descend the exceptional series via the hearing-group mechanism -- golden->2I=SL(2,5) and being->2T=SL(2,3) are EXACTLY the two exceptional isomorphisms where a binary polyhedral group is an SL(2,q); 2O is not one, so the E7 rung is structurally absent. The conjecture is true only at the FIELD level (Q(sqrt2)=char field of 2O=E7's McKay partner, verified in-cell), which is necessary-not-sufficient and E20-deflated (the order-16 dihedral group shares Q(sqrt2)); the hearing GROUP the object actually produces at conductor 8 is SL(2,Z/8), not 2O.
discriminating_fact: The object's hearing group at the silver conductor is <R,L> mod 8 = SL(2,Z/8), order 384 != 48=|2O|. The shadow mechanism gives a binary polyhedral group (unique involution) only at odd-prime conductors: SL(2,F3)=2T at 3 (E6), SL(2,F5)=2I at 5 (E8), each with exactly 1 involution. The silver conductor 8 is even: SL(2,Z/8) has 15 involutions (not binary polyhedral), and its unique order-48 natural quotient SL(2,Z/4) has order-histogram {1: 1, 2: 7, 3: 8, 4: 24, 6: 8} (0 order-8, 7 involutions) vs 2O's {1: 1, 2: 1, 3: 8, 4: 18, 6: 8, 8: 12} (12 order-8, 1 involution) -- not isomorphic. And 2O is not SL(2,q) for any q (|SL(2,q)|=q(q^2-1) is 24 at q=3, 120 at q=5, never 48). So the golden->2I/E8 match is one of exactly TWO exceptional isomorphisms 2T=SL(2,3), 2I=SL(2,5); there is no q for 2O/E7, so the descent breaks at the silver rung. Verify-don't-trust: the folklore '2O=GL(2,3)' is FALSE (GL(2,3) has 13 involutions; the two are non-isomorphic double covers of S4). Premise (Q(sqrt2)=char field of 2O [8 irreps, degs 1,1,2,2,2,3,3,4, all values in Q(sqrt2)], McKay quiver=affine E7) is TRUE but necessary-not-sufficient and shared with dihedral-16.
headline: the silver hearing group does NOT show 2O structure and CANNOT: its mod-8 shadow is SL(2,Z/8) (order 384, not 48); the only order-48 natural quotient SL(2,Z/4) is not 2O (it has zero order-8 elements and 7 involutions, whereas 2O has 12 order-8 elements and a UNIQUE involution -- the binary-polyhedral signature); and 2O is never SL(2,q) for any prime power q. The shadow mechanism yields a binary polyhedral group only at ODD-PRIME conductors (SL(2,F3)=2T at 3, SL(2,F5)=2I at 5, each with a unique involution); the silver conductor is 8 (even, =2^3), whose shadow SL(2,Z/8) has 15 involutions and is not binary polyhedral at all. So the metallic ladder does NOT descend the exceptional series via the hearing-group mechanism -- golden->2I=SL(2,5) and being->2T=SL(2,3) are EXACTLY the two exceptional isomorphisms where a binary polyhedral group is an SL(2,q); 2O is not one, so the E7 rung is structurally absent. The conjecture is true only at the FIELD level (Q(sqrt2)=char field of 2O=E7's McKay partner, verified in-cell), which is necessary-not-sufficient and E20-deflated (the order-16 dihedral group shares Q(sqrt2)); the hearing GROUP the object actually produces at conductor 8 is SL(2,Z/8), not 2O.
